mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding output and capacity utilization, which signals stronger corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations. When INDPRO exceeds 100, it confirms the economy is above pre-recession levels, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return for S&P 500 is negative or not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in periods when INDPRO is above 100, or if rank IC over any 3-year rolling window is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below the 8% threshold signals the labor market is healing decisively, boosting consumer confidence and spending. This improvement in the macro outlook drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return for S&P 500 is negative or not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in the 60 days following an UNRATE drop below 8%, or if the signal has a hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate robust demand from both housing and consumer sectors. This joint expansion signals accelerating economic activity that drives corporate profits and equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return for S&P 500 is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in instances where both HOUST exceeds 600 and RSAFS exceeds 370000, or if the interaction term is not significant in a regression (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: GDP growth in the aftermath of a severe recession provides the clearest signal that the economy is on a sustainable recovery path. Rising GDP confirms that demand is expanding, which lifts corporate revenues and equity valuations.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return for S&P 500 is negative or not significantly positive during the post-2009 recession expansion, or if the correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns is less than 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above the near-zero floor signals the end of emergency monetary accommodation. Markets interpret this as a tightening cycle that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return for S&P 500 is positive or not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in the 60 days following a rise in FEDFUNDS above 0.25%, or if the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate a strengthening labor market with rising employment and income. This supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return for S&P 500 is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when PAYEMS is rising (month-over-month increase), or if rank IC over any 3-year window is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8% and industrial production rises above 100, the economy is firmly in expansion. This joint condition confirms that both labor and manufacturing are strengthening, providing a powerful composite signal for positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return for S&P 500 is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in the 20 days following the joint trigger, or if the interaction effect in a regression is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, a moderate rise in consumer prices signals healthy demand growth without overheating. This is interpreted as a positive economic development that supports corporate pricing power and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return for S&P 500 is negative or not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when CPI YoY is below 3% and CPIAUCSL is rising, or if the correlation is below 0.1.
